TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD03_000:
Not sure which character to choose? You can check out their profiles from the Character Select screen.
There, you can watch move showcase videos and read helpful hints about how to play the character.
You'll be spending a lot of time with your character, so it makes sense to pick one that you can really get invested in.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_001: The key to mastering air combos in TEKKEN 8 is Tornado! Practice a sample combo.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_002: First off, try inputting a combo starter when you're ready.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_003: Don't worry about perfecting the combo straightaway, let's just try and perform the first two moves!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_004: Looks like they're hitting the ground before your next move connects with them. Try inputting the move just a little bit sooner.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_005: All right! You're reaching the tornado part of the combo now! You've almost got this!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_006: If you ever want to check out the exact timings for a combo, press {gui_view}+{gui_use} to see a demonstration of it.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_007: Once you get used to the inputs, you'll be pulling off perfect combos in no time!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_008: Yeah! You just did a Tornado that will send your opponent into a tailspin. Use Tornado to make landing air combos easier!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_009:
But real matches can be intense, and it can be all too easy to drop a combo.
That's why you gotta keep practicing, so that you never mess up, even under pressure!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_010:
In Practice Mode, you can try out tons of other combos too!
You should check it out whenever you wanna hone your skills further.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_011: Hey, I was watching your match from the sidelines. I reckon you'd be stronger if you practiced more air combos.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_012:
As it happens, I've got just the thing for perfecting air combos.
Wanna try it out?
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_013:
Listen to your partner's advice about air combos?
(Advice can be viewed at any time from the Partner Menu.)
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD05_015: It's all good. If you do get curious about air combos, feel free to ask anytime!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_001: High attacks and mid attacks won't do any damage to an opponent while they are in a standing block.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_002: And that's where low attacks come in! Perform a low attack on an opponent in a standing block to reduce their health.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_003: All right! Show me a low attack!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_005: Yo, that's not a low attack!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_006:
Hold up!
I was gonna teach you that next.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_007: So, you know about unblockable attacks, huh? I'm impressed!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_008:
Other than low attacks, throws can also be effective against a standing block!
You gotta be close to the opponent to pull them off, but they do some decent damage in return.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_009: All right! Show me a throw!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_011: Come on, that’s not a throw!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_012: Nice! Now you get how to win against an opponent who always has their defenses up!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_013:
So if your opponent is in a standing block, hit 'em with a low attack or a throw.
Let's try it for real now!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_014:
Hey, I was watching your match from the sidelines. I reckon you'd be stronger if you started using low attacks.
I could give you a few pointers if you want?
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_015:
Listen to your partner's advice about low attacks?
(Advice can be viewed at any time from the Partner Menu.)
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD06_017: It's all good. If you do get curious about low attacks, feel free to ask anytime.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_001:
You already know about Heat Engagers.
That means when you land a hit, you charge in while shifting into Heat state.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_002:
So you think it's a move you don't use once you've activated Heat?
Actually, there's another use for Heat Engagers.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_003: I want you to try out this method. With Heat activated, keep holding until Heat Engager hits!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_004:
Then you'll perform a Heat Dash!
You can move forward if they block and perform a follow-up attack if you land a hit!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_005: Isn't it great? Try it out for yourself!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_006:
Remember the first air combo I showed you?
It's the one where you knock them into the air then repeatedly press {gui_bank0_heat} for a combo into a Heat Smash.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_007:
Well, you can add that into a combo with the Heat Dash you just learned.
Use {gui_bank0_heat} to activate Heat, then immediately use Heat Dash to get the combo!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_008: Another combo route you can use is using a Tornado move after landing {gui_bank0_heat}.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_009:
You can only use a Tornado move once per combo.
But since {gui_bank0_heat} isn't a Tornado move, you can do that too.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_010: You can even do a combo that combines all the things you just learned! Strike with {gui_bank0_heat}, then add in Heat Dash and Tornado!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_011:
That delivers a lot of damage just by launching them in the air once.
It's a solid way to boost your Win Rate. Give it a try!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_012:
Wow! You already mastered that super-powerful combo!
I was right about you!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_013:
Now there's nothing you need to fear!
Just keep piling on the combos in battle.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_014: There ya go! That's a Heat Dash!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_015:
It's an unusual command, so maybe you're not used to it yet.
Try doing the same Heat Dash again!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_017:
You can use Heat Dash whenever you're in a Heat state.
But activating it consumes 1 Heat Energy.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_018: The amount of Heat Energy you have is displayed here on the screen.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_019:
You gain 1 Heat Energy when you use Heat Burst, and 2 when you use a Heat Engager!
Hit with a Heat Engager and you can do Heat Dash twice!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_020: The way to send opponents flying changes when using Heat Dash. When you land a hit, aim for a follow-up attack!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_021: If you're finding it a bit tough, you can always hold off for a bit and come back to it later. But if you manage to master it, you'll have an even bigger, fiercer repertoire of tactics to pull from!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_022: You did it! Amazing! With this new skill under your belt, you're sure to do even more damage while in a Heat state!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_023:
Now you've done it once, time to practice some more to get it down!
Try the same Heat Dash combo again!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_024:
Nice!
You've mastered air combos that use Heat Dash!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_025: Now you're sure to do more damage while you're in a Heat state! Try it against an opponent in the arcade.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD12_026: All right! Let's try it out in battle!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_001: Not getting up quickly when you take a hit and get knocked to the ground can mean serious trouble for you.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_002: When you get knocked down, your opponent will try and hit you with follow-up attacks to deal even more damage.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_003: You can perform a side roll by pressing certain buttons the moment you hit the ground. It'll help you avoid incoming attacks, so you'll wanna remember this.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_003_XB: You can perform a side roll by pressing certain buttons the moment you hit the ground. It'll help you avoid incoming attacks, so you'll wanna remember this.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_004:
All right, let's try out some ukemi!
After getting hit, press one of the buttons repeatedly!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_004_XB:
All right, let's try out some ukemi!
After getting hit, press one of the buttons repeatedly!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_006: It's easier to do if you repeatedly press the button before you hit the ground!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_006_XB: It's easier to do if you repeatedly press the button before you hit the ground!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_007:
But you can't just keep side rolling either, you know?
If your opponent predicts that you're going to roll, they can put more pressure on you with a well-timed attack.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_008: In situations like that, you can fake a side roll with a wake-up attack!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_009: Now, let's try to perform a wake-up attack! Press for a mid kick, or for a low kick.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_010: Land a hit with a wake-up attack
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_011: You won't be able to avoid an attack performed at a different timing to your roll.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_012: Yeah, nice! You can utilize ukemi and attack by pressing . Finding the right time to use it can mean the difference between winning and losing out there!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_013:
Looks like you're a little late pressing the button.
Keep pressing the kick button as soon as you're downed.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_013_XB:
Looks like you're a little late pressing the button.
Keep pressing the kick button as soon as you're knocked down.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_014:
Whoa! You don't see that kind of counter often!
That might actually be a pretty good strategy in a real match.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_015: Once you've got ukemi and wake-up attacks down, you'll find that you won't be at such a disadvantage anymore when you get knocked down!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_016:
I was scoping out your match...
Learning all the different ways to get up should put you on the right path.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_017: I could teach you a thing or two about ukemi and wake-up attacks. What d'ya say?
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_018:
Listen to your partner's advice about ground techniques and wake-up attacks?
(Advice can be viewed at any time from the Partner Menu.)
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD13_020: It's all good. If you do get curious about ukemi and wake-up attacks, feel free to ask anytime.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_001: If you wanna be the strongest out there, it's not enough to just know yourself, you gotta know your enemy too.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_002: The best way to uncover your opponent's weaknesses is to master punishment techniques.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_003: Some of the opponent's moves will leave them open after you block them.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_004:
During this momentary opening, they won't be able to block.
This is your chance to attack!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_005: Deal damage with a punishment technique after blocking
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_006: Block their attack, then hit them with a punishment technique!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_007: You can press the button even earlier, you know!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_007_XB: You can press the button even earlier, you know!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_008:
That move's gonna be tricky to punish with.
I reckon a faster move will get the job done.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_009:
That's a legit strategy in a real match, for sure!
For now, let's practice punishing after blocking, okay?
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_010:
Looks like you've got a handle on punishments.
Nice work!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_011:
When you've mastered punishment techniques, you can even turn your opponent's attacks into chances for you to attack.
Learn each character's move animations, and strike back after blocking!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_012:
Speaking of which, did you know that the length of an opening will depend on the move?
Moves with longer openings will allow you to perform even more damaging punishments!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_013:
See, some punishments even allow you to start an air combo.
Making use of chances like this are a surefire way to rack up more wins!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_014:
Oh yeah, looking good!
I think you're ready for the real thing now!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_015:
Looking to sneak in a bit more punishment practice?
There's a menu in Practice Mode just for that; you should check it out!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD19_016: If you're getting beat up by the same character every time, you're likely missing out on opportunities to punish them. If you can master punishment techniques, there won't be anyone out there you can't beat!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_001: Whip out Heat Burst to trigger Heat! When you want to get aggressive, activate Heat and go after your opponent!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_002:
Every character has a move which activates Heat when it hits!
This is what I recommend: go in and attack with this!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_003: When you activate Heat, don't forget Heat Smash! Attacking with Heat Smash will wipe out your opponent's health in no time flat!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_004:
Learn the air combos too!
Knock your opponent into the air with this move, then you can strike them as many times as you like until they hit the ground!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_005:
You should learn one more move that's easy to use in battle!
Fall back on it any time you're in doubt.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_006:
You should learn one more move that's easy to use in battle!
Fall back on it any time you're in doubt.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_007:
Learn Tornado, which is a vital air combo in TEKKEN 8!
It's important for making follow-up attacks easier to land.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_008:
When you land specific attacks, they drop your opponent in a position that's vulnerable to follow-up attacks!
This is the Tornado. It helps you string air combos together.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_009:
Once you catch them with a Tornado, you can add any follow-up attack you like.
Maybe you could even go with the Heat Smash you practiced earlier.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_010:
All right, you've learned all the moves you need.
Now you're ready for a real battle!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_011: Good job! That's Heat Burst! Use Heat Burst in battle to trigger Heat and go on the offensive!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_012:
Amazing! You've mastered Heat Smash!
Remember, when you go into a Heat state, attack with a Heat Smash!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_013: Ready to start using it in battle? It's okay if you need a bit more practice first.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_014: Wanna try the Battle Tutorial again?
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_015: Okay! We can keep doing this until you're comfortable with it.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD30_016:
All right!
Time for your first battle. You got this!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_000_002: Air combos that use the Heat System
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_001: Got a feel for using Heat Smash now? There's actually a surefire way to land your Heat Smash...
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_002:
And that's with an air combo!
An air combo is when you knock your opponent into the air and then use follow-up attacks.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_003:
An opponent in the air is defenseless, so your Heat Smash is guaranteed to hit.
TEKKEN just wouldn't be TEKKEN without air combos. Try one now!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_004:
Still a bit tricky?
You can skip this with {gui_menu}, then try again when you feel ready!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_005: You did it! Now you can do air combos!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_006: There's nothing quite like dishing out serious damage with air combos. Now you're ready to be a true TEKKEN player!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_007:
Feel ready to nail an air combo in battle?
It's okay if you need a bit more practice first.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_008: Sure thing! We can keep practicing until you're used to it.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_009: Ooh, good attitude! Now go land an air combo in battle!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_ADVICE_AD43_010:
That wasn't just a fluke, right?
Show me you can do an air combo one more time.

TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_000:
First, try moving and attacking.
The ghost will copy your movements.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_001: Look at that! The ghost is learning at an amazing rate!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_002: Ah, of course. It'll learn air combos and Heat as well, so give them a try!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_003:
If you don't block then the ghost won't learn to block either.
If you start blocking, the ghost will also learn to punish!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_004: It's really learned a lot now! It can also learn Rage Arts and Special Style attacks!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_005:
It's a complex feeling, defeating your Ghost.
But then you don't want to lose to it either!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_006:
Continue to fight your Ghost and it will continue to learn.
It might be fun for the two of you to get stronger together! Try it again soon!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_008:
Ghosts are characters created based on AI learning of your own tendencies as a player.
They are created automatically as you play the online modes and Arcade Quest.
However, the following types of battle are not used as targets for learning.
- Online battles that don't have the default number of rounds/battle time.
- Tutorial sections during Arcade Quest.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_010:
Super Ghost Battles allow you to enjoy battles against the following types of Ghost.
- Your own Ghost
- Downloaded Ghosts
- CPU Ghosts
The CPU Ghosts were created from play data provided by the developers of the game.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_GHOST_012:
- A Ghost is saved for each character.
- Ghosts can be fought against after they have learned from a certain number of battles.
- Ghosts are automatically uploaded to the server after every 3 online matches.
- Ghosts will be deleted from the server if you don't go online for 30 days.

Report unsportsmanlike behavior or problematic user-generated content.
Choose the corresponding category for your report.
Note: Making false reports is a punishable offense.
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_017: Content including offensive or hate speech
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_022: Spam or content that invites misunderstandings
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_034:
Please describe the unsportsmanlike behavior here.
Furthermore, note that reports made through this form do not receive replies.
Those requesting replies should submit inquiries through Customer Support.
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_035:
- Date and time you experienced the unsportsmanlike behavior
(Please make sure to denote AM or PM for the time.)

- Place you experienced the unsportsmanlike behavior

- Who was the target of the unsportsmanlike behavior

- Details of the unsportsmanlike behavior

- Any additional information
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_039: You have reached the daily report limit, and cannot submit any more reports.

TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_053:
Cheating, harassment, posting inappropriate content,
and other unsportsmanlike behavior violates the rules of the EULA.
The "Report" function exists to report these kinds of behavior, so that everyone can enjoy a safe gaming experience.
If the admin team deems the unsportsmanlike behavior to be severe, it may take appropriate action,
including restricting the use of some functions, and deleting the offending content.
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_056: Sharing personal info; intent to meet in real life
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_069: Playing with the intent to harass others
TEXT_000_UI_REPORT_075: Intention to meet in real life

TEXT_000_UI_BLOCK_030:
Block is a function to restrict play and communication with others who you have blocked. All of the following functions are restricted.
The person you block does not receive any notification that you have blocked them.
TEXT_000_UI_BLOCK_032: Communication, such as text chats, are restricted.
TEXT_000_UI_BLOCK_034:
- In some modes, matching is prohibited.
- Blocked players are prohibited from entering into sessions you have created.
TEXT_000_UI_BLOCK_036:
- Blocked players cannot send free texts.
- Customized characters created by blocked players are blocked.
TEXT_000_UI_BLOCK_038:
There is no difference between the TEKKEN 8 block list and the platform's block list in terms of the restricted functions. However, note that the block lists are handled separately.
For example, adding someone to the TEKKEN 8 block list does not automatically add them to the platform's block list.
TEXT_000_UI_BLOCK_039: - Blocked players cannot send free texts.

Block this player?
(They will be removed from your friend list.)

TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_025:
There are arcade machines in the Battle Area.
If you sit at a vacant machine, you can battle against other players.
The types of machines differ between battle modes.
TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_026:
You can play Ranked Matches on a Singles machine.
With this machine, you can also match up with players in other lounges.
TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_027:
On a Group Match machine, you can play with players in the same lounge.
You will be matched with players sitting at adjacent machines.
If three or more players are participating, matching is random from among the participants.
TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_028:
If you select a machine in which another player is already battling, you can watch the battle.
When the match starts, you will be taken automatically to the Battle Screen to start watching.
TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_032:
This mode lets you create player match sessions and battle within them.
Choose whether to create or search for a session.
TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_035:
Antenna Icon: Reflects the network environment and connection between
you and your opponent.
{cmd_wireless_full} indicates you are connected via Wi-Fi,
and {cmd_antenna_full} indicates you are on a wired connection.
The more bars there are, the better your connection is.
Please note that Wi-Fi connections can be less stable than wired ones and
may affect your opponent as well.
TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_036:
RTT: The response time for communications between you and your opponent.
This number shows the total time between sending and receiving communications.
Units are in milliseconds.
Delay Frames: The delay between frames. Units are 1/60 of a second.
Rollback Frames: The number of frames to be rolled back.
Units are 1/60 of a second.
TEXT_000_UI_MATCHING_037:
Processing Load Icon: Indicates how much of a computer's resources {btn_Platform_Steam} is
using for PC players.
On {cmd_delay_LR}, the left indicates your processing load, and the right is your opponent's.
When {cmd_delay_L} is red, there is a slowdown. When {cmd_delay_none_L} is green,
everything is running smoothly.

TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TITLE_SUBCATEGORY: カスタマイズマーケット
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_001:
Welcome to the Tekken Fight Lounge!
The Tekken Fight Lounge is a place where players gather from around the world.
Here, you can meet and battle against other players online.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_002_cbt:
In the <term>Battle Area</>, you can participate in online battles.
Battle it out to improve your rank in a <highlight>Ranked Match</>, battle against players
in a lounge in a <highlight>Group Match</>, or <highlight>spectate</> matches of other players.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_002:
In the Battle Area, you can participate in online battles.
Battle it out to improve your rank in Ranked Match, battle against players
in a lounge in Group Match, or spectate matches of other players.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_007:
Using Match Anywhere with {gui_rt} even when outside of the Battle Area will
allow you to search for opponents.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_010:
While close to an avatar, press {gui_decide} and select Ghost Match to battle a 
ghost created from that player's match data.

Now then, enjoy all the Tekken Fight Lounge has to offer!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_006:
An avatar is a player's in-game persona.
You will be able to move your avatar around in the lounge, as well as use
a variety of features.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_008:
<highlight>Control your avatar</> with {gui_up}{gui_down}{gui_left}{gui_right} and head to all sorts of places!
You can also <highlight>control the camera</> with {btn_RS}.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_02_001:
You can move to other lounges by selecting Move Lounges from the Lounge Menu.

-Recommended
Enter a recommended lounge.

-Select from Friend List
You can select and enter a lounge that your friends, rivals, and followers are in.

-Select by Region
You can select and enter a lounge by region.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_02_001_cbt:
You can move to other lounges by selecting Move Lounges from the Lounge Menu.

-Recommended
Enter a recommended lounge.

-Select by Region
You can select and enter a lounge by region.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_08_001_cbt:
You can bring up Emote Shortcuts with {gui_detail}.
Select an emote from these shortcuts to have your avatar perform the action.
Avatar actions can be seen by other players.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_08_001:
You can bring up Emote Shortcuts with {gui_detail}.
Select an emote from these shortcuts to have your avatar perform the action.
You can register eight emotes as shortcuts, and switch in others from the list.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_08_002:
You can switch the emotes registered as your Emote Shortcuts.
Pick your favorites from the list and register them as shortcuts!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_02_001:
The <term>Chat</> function can be accessed by using {gui_use} to open a chat window.
You can enter the text of your choice, as well as use <highlight>stickers</>
and <highlight>set phrases</> to communicate with other players.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_02_002:
Use stickers and set phrases for casual conversation.
Both are shown in the language used by the person receiving your chat.
The Favorites tab also shows all of your most recent choices.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_02_003:
By switching to the <term>Chat tab</>, you can check <highlight>chat histories by categories</>.
To see the entire chat history, select the <highlight>History (All) tab</>.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_02_004:
You can use a keyboard or the on-screen keyboard to enter messages.
Make the chat window active and press {gui_decide} to then freely enter text.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_03_001:
<term>Lounge Chat</> is a <highlight>way of sending a message to all players in the same lounge</>.
However, depending on your <caution>status (e.g. during a match), there is a possibility that you may not be able to read it</>.
When you send a message to the lounge, a speech bubble will appear above your avatar's head which is visible to nearby players.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_04_001:
<term>Nearby Chat</> is a <highlight> way of sending a message to all nearby players</>.
The circular area displayed is a rough estimate of the area the message sent to if you <highlight>send the message to nearby</>.
When you send a message to nearby players, a speech bubble will appear above your avatar's head which is visible to nearby players.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_05_001:
Direct Chat is a private chat feature.
You will also be able to send messages to players in different lounges.
You can't chat with players who aren't in a lounge.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_06_001:
Session Chat is a chat that you can do during some matches, such as player matches.
You can still view the chat history after returning to the lounge, but 
chatting is only available during the session.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_22_001:
Using <term>Match Anywhere</> with {gui_rt} will <highlight>allow you to search for opponents</>.
However, unlike when searching from a match ring, <caution>it will not let you practice</>
<caution>during standby</>.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_10_001:
In the Lounge Menu, you can move in and out of lounges and read Help text.
You can also change the game settings.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_10_002:
From <term>Move</>, you can select the area of your choice to <highlight>move there instantly</>.
You can move within your lounge, or even <highlight>move to another lounge</>.
You can also return to the Main Menu from here.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_19_001_cbt:
Profile lets you check your and other players' info, as well as your play style.
You can also add obtained titles, avatars, and characters in any combination
that you want.
(You cannot edit your comment during the CBT.)
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_19_001:
Profile lets you check your and other players' info, as well as your play style.
You can also add obtained titles, avatars, and characters in any combination
that you want.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_19_002:
When viewing other players' profiles, press {gui_use} to <highlight>compare play styles</>
between you and them.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_16_001:
The Follow List lets you follow up to 100 of your favorite players.
You can follow or unfollow a player from each list or profile, and check
the info of followed players from the Follow List.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_16_002:
Followed players are marked by a {cmd_follow} above their head.
(The icons for rivals and friends have the highest display priority.)

Following is a one-way process, and is not conveyed to the followed player.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_17_001_cbt:
Players who get along can agree to become friends.
You can send and approve friend requests from the each list or profile,
and you can check related information on your friend list.
You can register or send requests to up to 100 people, and each friend will
have a special icon floating above their heads.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_17_001:
Players who get along can agree to become friends.
You can send and approve friend requests from each list or profile,
and you can check related information on your friend list.
You can register or send requests to up to 100 people.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_17_002:
Friends can send each other Player Match invitations, and 
view friend info in the battle results.
Friends will have a {cmd_friend} floating above their heads.
(Rival icons have the highest display priority.)
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_18_001:
You can register only one rival from your favorite players.
You can register or deregister your rival from any list or profile.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_18_002:
After registering a rival, their info will be displayed in the battle results.
Rivals will also have a {cmd_rival} floating above their heads.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_14_001:
You can <highlight>inspect or interact with other players in your lounge</>.
From here, you can view their profiles or send them friend requests.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_15_001:
You can check Player Match notifications sent from friends.
Select a notification from the list to join that session.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_20_001:
Once you've encountered players in a Ranked Match or Group Match,
you'll be able to view information on them.
From there, you can send a friend request or view their profile.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BTL_01_001:
In the Battle Area, you can participate in online battles.
You'll find all sorts of machines or "Rings" you can use, including those for Ranked Matches and Group Matches.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BTL_01_002_cbt:
In the Single Ring, you can play Ranked Match or Quick Match bouts.
In Ranked Match, battle with players around the world to improve your rank.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BTL_01_002:
In the <term>Single Ring</>, you can play in either <highlight>Ranked Match</> or <highlight>Quick Match</> bouts.
In a Ranked Match, you can challenge players around the world to <highlight>improve your rank</>.
In a Quick Match, you can <highlight>fight without worrying about your rank</>.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BTL_01_004:
In the Group Match Ring, you can participate in Group Match battles against players in the lounge.
Group Matches will match machines that depict the same character.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BTL_01_005:
Press {gui_decide} next to a player in a match to spectate.
Watch the matches of any players who catch your eye!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BTL_01_005_01:
Press {gui_decide} close to a player in a match to spectate that match.
Watch matches of any players that catch your eye!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTM_01_001:
In the Customization Shop, 
players can customize their avatars: their other selves.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTM_01_002:
Customization can be accessed by talking to a <highlight>shop clerk</> or accessing a <highlight>terminal</>.
Whether you talk to a shop clerk or access a terminal, you will be presented with the same options.
Customize your avatar and more to your liking!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_DOJO_01_001:
In the Tekken Dojo, you can play in Practice Mode.
In Practice Mode, you can practice inputs and combos without the pressure of
a match weighing you down. It's the perfect place to challenge yourself to play
characters you aren't familiar with yet.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_DOJO_01_002:
In the Training Ring, you can practice by yourself.
Practice moves while looking at the Move List or take on combo challenges.
You can also set the CPU to remember a string of commands to repeatedly perform
for you to train against.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_DOJO_01_003:
In Train Together, you can practice with another player.
Sit down in the Training Ring to train together with your friend.
Recommended for strategizing or research that's hard to pull off by yourself.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BEACH_01_001: In the Beach Area, you can play Tekken Ball with players in your lounge.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BEACH_01_002:
Play Tekken Ball by sitting at the machine in the Beach Area.
You can also spectate by pressing {gui_decide} next to a player in a match.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_BEACH_01_002_01:
Play Tekken Ball by sitting at the machine in the Beach Area.
You can also spectate a player in a match by pressing {gui_decide} close to them.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TITLE_CNT_01_001_: In the Full Version of Tekken Dojo
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CNT_01_001_cbt:
The Tekken Dojo is a practice area.
Practice Mode will be available in the full version of the game.
Future updates will allow you to practice together with your friends.
We hope you are looking forward to it!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TITLE_CNT_02_001_: In the Full Version of Beach Area
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CNT_02_001_cbt:
The Beach Area is where you can play Tekken Ball online.
We hope to have Tekken Ball available via the in-game arcade machines
in the full version of the game.
We hope you are looking forward to it!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_003_cbt:
In the Customization Shop, you can customize your avatar.
Stop on in whenever your avatar needs a makeover!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_003:
In the Customization Shop, you can customize battle characters or your avatar.
You can also upload or purchase customized items.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_004:
In the Tekken Dojo, you can play in Practice Mode.
Train by yourself, or bring a friend along to train together.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_005:
In the Beach Area, you can play Tekken Ball.
This aggressive version of beach ball can be played with characters from the
roster for some lighthearted fun.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_LOUNGE_01_009:
Use chats with {gui_use}, and use emotes with {gui_detail}.
Both are good ways to communicate with players in your lounge.

Now then, enjoy all the Tekken Fight Lounge has to offer!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_01_001:
プレイヤーの分身となる存在です。
TEKKEN FIGHT LOUNGEではアバターを操作する事で

TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_CTRL_01_003:
Rスティックでカメラの向きを変更することができます。
上下左右に向きを変更できます。

TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_017: Action After a Hit or Block
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_172: Command inputs displayed below are for characters facing right.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_191: Exit game and return to the Main Menu?
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_192: Set a punishment technique from Punishment Settings.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_198:
Punishment Training

By identifying your opponent's openings after you block their most-used attacks, you can then practice techniques that can hit them without the risk of being blocked.

- Normal -
In this mode, your opponent will perform the same attack until you are able to successfully punish them. A recommended punishment technique will be displayed before their attacks.

- Expert -
In this mode, each attack your opponent performs will be randomly selected. No recommended punishment technique will be displayed until you block.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_200: Use the current input delay settings outside of practice?
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_201:
Frame Data

You can choose between "Show Details", "Simple Display", and "Show Frame Advantage".

"Show Details" displays frames in numerical units for your characters' actions. (A "frame" is a unit of measurement used to indicate the passage of time in the game. 1 frame is 1/60 of a second.)

"Simple Display" changes your character's color according to the frame advantage between you and the opponent. Grey shows recovery time, blue indicates when you have the frame advantage, and red indicates when you are at a disadvantage. When the difference is 10 frames or greater, the shade of the color will be deeper.

"Show Frame Advantage" displays only the frame advantage between you and the opponent in numerical units.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_202:
Show Details (1)

Attack Start-up Frames
Shows the frame count at which an attack connects with an opponent. The duration of the hit (in frames) is displayed within the parentheses ( ).

Frame Advantage
Shows the difference in the amount of time each character is unable to act in terms of frames. The frame advantage based on ukemi timing is displayed in the parentheses ( ).
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_203:
Show Details (2)

Status
Displays the character's status.
Statuses include: Standing, Crouching, Airborne, Down, Invincible, and Power Crush. Each status affects behavior upon hit and whether or not an attack will land.

Distance from Opponent
Displays your character's distance from the opponent. With this information, you can observe the reach of each technique and learn how far you'll be from the opponent after an attack is blocked.

Power Crush
A move that can absorb mid and high attacks.

Homing Attack

TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_239:

No Need for Complex Settings!
Recommended Training to Dive Right In
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_517:
■Combo Challenges■
Practice basic combos. Learn combo starters and follow-ups that'll let you whittle away your opponent's health in no time flat.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_518:
■Punishment Training■
Practice punishments against your opponent's most commonly used attacks. Identify openings and punish them with the appropriate techniques to deal damage without risk of being blocked.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_519:
- Normal -
In this mode, your opponent will perform the same attack until you are able to successfully punish them. A recommended punishment technique will be displayed before their attacks.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_520:
- Expert -
In this mode, each attack your opponent performs will be randomly selected. No recommended punishment technique will be displayed until you block.

TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_566:
The following practice features are great for anyone who's wondering how to play a character.
- Punishment Training
- Combo Challenges
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_567:
Punishment Training
Practice one of the fundamentals of a good defense: punishing after blocking.
Identify openings and punish your opponent with the appropriate techniques to deal damage without the risk of your opponent blocking your moves.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_568:
Combo Challenges
Practice one of the fundamentals of dealing damage: combos.
Learn combo starters and follow-ups that'll let you whittle away your opponent's health in no time flat.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_569:
You can view all of a character's moves via the Move List.
Press {gui_lb} or {gui_rb} to switch between All Techniques and Main Techniques.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_570:
The Main Technique Move List displays a character's most important moves.
You can find information about what each move is used for, its effects, and other moves it is often used with.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_571:
The Move List can be displayed while training.
Press {gui_detail} while viewing the Move List to display it while you practice.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_571_XB:
The Move List can be displayed while training.
Press {gui_detail} while viewing the Move List to display it while you practice.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_572:
Moves which have extra information are marked with an icon at the bottom right.
You can view this information by selecting the move in the Move List, or by displaying the move on screen as you practice.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_573:
Moves with special properties are marked with an icon at the top right.
Press {gui_use} while viewing the Move List to see what each icon stands for.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_574: Attack Info displays information regarding attacks landed on the opponent.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_575:
Total Damage
Displays the total amount of damage dealt to the training opponent over the course of playing in Practice Mode.
Max Combo Damage
Displays the highest damaging combo performed on the training opponent over the course of playing in Practice Mode.
Hit Properties
Displays a move's Hit Properties. Moves with special properties are marked with an icon.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_576:
Damage
Displays the damage dealt to the training opponent and the percentage of health reduced by a move. Rage state, counter hits, and midair hits will affect this percentage.
Recoverable Damage
Displays the remaining damage that can be recovered. Blocking certain moves and midair hits can recover a portion of the damage that has been dealt.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_577:
Clean
Flashes when a move with a clean hit effect hits at close range.
Counter
Flashes when a move hits just before an opponent's move does.
Punish
Flashes when a move hits your opponent when they leave an opening after an attack.
Wall
Flashes when your opponent hits a wall.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_578:
Frame Data displays a variety of information about the moves you perform.
Show Details allows you to check detailed information, Simple Display uses colors to indicate when characters have a frame advantage/disadvantage, and Frame Advantage just shows the difference in recovery time.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_579:
- Show Details -
Attack Start-up Frames
These represent the times at which an attack's hitbox becomes active.
Displays frames upon hitting an opponent (Attack Start – Attack End).
Frame Advantage
Displays the difference in the amount of time each character is unable to act (in number of frames).
Displays the frame advantage between both characters (based on the opponent's ukemi recovery).
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_580:
Status
Displays the character's status.
Statuses include: Standing, Crouching, Airborne, Down, Invincible, and Power Crush. Crouching will evade high attacks, and low attacks will not hit an airborne character (only if the "Airborne" letters appear blue).
Distance from Opponent
Displays your character's distance from the opponent. You are particularly vulnerable when your attack misses your opponent, so with this information, you can observe the reach of each technique to ensure your attacks always connect.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_581:
- Simple Display -
Your character will light up blue when they recover first, and red when your opponent recovers first.
Your character will light up white when neither fighter is at an advantage/disadvantage.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_582:
- Frame Advantage -
Only displays the frame advantage from Show Details.
Displays the frame advantage between both characters (based on the opponent's ukemi recovery).
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_583:
Practice one of the fundamentals of a good defense: punishing after blocking.
Identify openings and punish your opponent with the appropriate techniques to deal damage without the risk of your opponent blocking your moves.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_584:
Normal
- In this mode, your opponent will perform the same attack until you are able to successfully punish them.
- A recommended punishment technique will be displayed before their attacks.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_585:
Expert
- In this mode, each attack your opponent performs will be performed at random.
- No recommended punishment technique will be displayed until you block.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_586:

Normal

TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_648:
Wondering what a character's best moves are? What their combos look like?
What their optimal punishment techniques are? Check the following to learn
how to use a character to their fullest potential.
- Main Techniques
- Combo Challenges
- Punishment Training
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_649:
The Main Techniques Move List shows popular techniques, move properties,
and useful sequences. Check out Main Techniques if you want to find out
how to use a character.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_650:
In Combo Challenge, practice one of the fundamentals of dealing damage: combos.
Use combo starters and follow-ups to deal huge damage to your opponent.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_651:
In Punishment Training, practice punishing your opponent's moves.
Your opponent will only use moves with large openings. Practice punishing these
moves after blocking them.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_652:
The Move List displays the following information:
- Move names
- Commands
- Additional information
- Special properties
- Demo playbacks
- Main Techniques (popular techniques, move properties, useful sequences)
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_653:
Moves with additional information are marked with an icon at the bottom right.
Details can be seen by moving your cursor over the icon.

Moves with special properties are marked with an icon at the top right.
You can find out what each icon stands for by pressing {gui_use} while viewing
the Move List, or by checking out Viewing the Move List in Help.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_654:
You can see what a move looks like with Play Demo.
This is a useful feature if you're unsure whether you're performing a move correctly.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_655: The Move List can be displayed while training. Press {gui_detail} while viewing the Move List to display it while you practice.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_655_XB: The Move List can be displayed while training. Press {gui_detail} while viewing the Move List to display it while you practice.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_656:
Attack Info displays the following information:
- Total Damage
- Max Combo Damage
- Hit Properties
- Damage
- Recoverable Damage
- Hit Info
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_657:
Total Damage
Displays the total amount of damage dealt to the training opponent.

Max Combo Damage
Displays the highest damaging combo performed on the training opponent.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_658:
Hit Properties
Displays the hit properties of a move when hitting an opponent.
Moves include highs, mids, lows, special mids, special lows, and unblockables.
Highs won't hit a crouching opponent and lows won't hit an opponent mid-jump.
Special mids and special lows can be blocked with a standing or crouching block.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_659:
Hit Properties
Moves with special properties are marked with an icon.
Check Hit Property Icons in Help for more information.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_660:
Damage
Displays the damage dealt to your opponent and damage scaling.
Rage state, counter hits, and midair hits will affect the amount
of damage scaling.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_661:
Recoverable Damage
Displays the amount of Recoverable Gauge remaining and damage scaling.
The Recoverable Gauge will appear after dealing damage to an airborne or downed
opponent and when the opponent blocks your attacks when you are in a Heat state.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_662:
Clean
Flashes when a move with a clean hit effect hits at close range.

Counter
Flashes when a move hits your opponent before their move hits you.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_663:
Punish
Flashes when you hit your opponent when they leave an opening after an attack.

Wall
Flashes when your opponent hits a wall.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_664:
Frame Data displays the following information:
- Start-Up Frames
- Frame Advantage
- Status
- Distance from Opponent

This can be displayed in three different ways.
- Show Details provides the most detailed information.
- Simple Display uses colors to indicate when characters have a frame
advantage/disadvantage.
- Frame Advantage shows only the difference in recovery time.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_665:
This can be displayed in three different ways.
- Show Details provides the most detailed information.
- Simple Display uses colors to indicate when characters have a frame
advantage/disadvantage.
- Frame Advantage just shows the difference in recovery time.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_666:
Attack Start-up Frames
Displays the times at which a move's hitbox becomes active
and the frames upon hitting an opponent (Hit Start – Hit End).
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_667:
Frame Advantage
Displays the difference in time each character has before they are able to act.
Displays who has the frame advantage (based on the opponent's ukemi recovery).
Blue letters represent an advantage, and red letters represent a disadvantage.
In Simply Display characters will light up blue when they are at an advantage, or red when they are at a disadvantage.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_668:
Status
Displays any character status that affects whether they can be hit.
 Statuses include: Standing, Crouching, Airborne, Down, Invincible,
and Power Crush. Crouching will evade high attacks, while low attacks
will not hit an airborne character (only if the letters "Airborne" appear in blue).
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_669:
Distance from Opponent
Displays your character's distance from the opponent.
Check a move's reach and the distance between characters after it's blocked.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_670:
In Punishment Training you can practice punishment techniques on opponent's
moves that have large openings.

Punishment Training has Normal and Expert difficulties.
Normal
- In this mode, your opponent will perform the same attack until you are able to
successfully punish them.
- A recommended punishment technique will be displayed before their attacks.

Expert
- In this mode, each attack your opponent performs will be randomly selected.
- A recommended punishment technique will be displayed after you block.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_671:
In Combo Challenge, practice one of the fundamentals of dealing damage.
Being able to correctly perform follow-up attacks after a combo starter is
a great way to deal huge damage to your opponent's health.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_672:
Press {gui_view}+{gui_use} for Play Demo to check a combo when you're not getting it right.
Factors like input timing, using incorrect moves, and needing a dash between
moves can prevent you from landing a combo successfully.
By checking the Command History in Play Demo, you might be able to spot where
you're going wrong.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_673:
Restart allows you to practice from a set position, direction, or status.
For example, if you want to practice wall combos, you can set Restart Position
to Wall for more efficient practice sessions.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_674:
You can restart with your back against a wall by setting the Restart Position to
Wall and then enabling Switch Positions.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_675:
You can use Status Record to restart from a particular moment in time.
By pressing {gui_rsb}, you can Remember a situation you want to start from,
and then press {gui_lsb} to Reproduce it, restarting you in that situation.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_676:
Set the opponent's punish action.
- Practice Mode > Offense
- Action After a Hit or Block > Punish

You can use this setting to check the following situations:
- Whether you can act faster than the opponent's punish action after they block
your move.
- Whether you can sidestep an opponent's punish action after they block
your move.
- Whether you can land a counter hit move on your opponent's punish action after
they are hit by your move.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_677:
You can set the opponent's punish action via Punishment Settings in 
Practice Settings.

Set the opponent's status before their punish action. Set the opponent's status
to Standing Punish or Crouching Punishment to have them stand or crouch
before attacking, respectively. Next, set the punish action. You can select a
popular technique from the Punishment List. Or, if you want the opponent
to perform an action not in the Punishment List, use Record to control
the character and set your own punish action.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_678:
You can set up to eight different punish actions each for Standing and Crouching
for the opponent. The frequency at which they perform each punish action can be
set via Action Frequency.

Your opponent won't just use one move during
a real match, so it's important to
practice a variety of situations.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_679: When this icon is displayed, your opponent can perform an action from a crouch.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_680:
When this icon is displayed, hitting the opponent against a wall will trigger
a Wall Break. Generally, moves that knock the opponent flying will trigger this,
but you will need to be very close to a wall in order to trigger a Wall Break.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_681:
When this icon is displayed, hitting the opponent against the ground will
trigger a Floor Break. Generally, moves that slam an airborne opponent
to the ground will trigger this.

TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_739:
The Heat System is one of TEKKEN 8's core battle systems.

- Heat activation can be performed once per round.
- Heat activation provides you with various effects and creates an opportunity to attack aggressively.
- Heat ends when the Heat Timer runs out.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_740:
There are two ways to activate Heat.

(1) Use Heat Burst.
(2) Hit your opponent with a Heat Engager.
However, this does not include hitting an airborne or downed opponent.

If Heat is already active or if your Heat Timer has been used up, then you cannot activate Heat.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_741: Heat Burst is a technique that allows you to activate Heat whenever you like. - Input the command and you will activate Heat and perform an attack at the same time. - It has the same properties as a Power Crush and can absorb high or mid attacks. - It cannot be defended against with attack reversals, parries, or Power Crushes. - It will also consume some of your Heat Timer. - You can press to cancel the attack part. - Any combo performed after landing a Heat Burst will leave the entirety of its damage as recoverable health. - You will be able to move again sooner than the opponent upon hit or guard.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_742:
A Heat Engager is a technique that will activate Heat only if it hits your opponent.

- Successful Heat activation will then move you in close to the opponent.
You will also regain some of your recoverable health.
- After moving in closer, you will have a significant advantage in when you can move again compared to your opponent.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_743:
After Heat activation, the Heat Timer displayed beneath your Health Gauge will start to decrease.
The Heat Timer displays how long your Heat status has left.

- The Heat Timer decreases as time passes.
- Once the Heat Timer runs out, Heat will end.
- If you are hit by your opponent's attack, your Heat Timer will be reduced according to the damage taken.
- The Heat Timer does not decrease when you are attacking.
- The Heat Timer does not decrease when your opponent is downed.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_744:
Heat has the following properties.

- You will inflict damage (chip damage) to your opponent when they block your attacks.
- You can use Heat Dash, a powerful way to start combos and offensive sequences.
- You can use the high-damage Heat Smash.
- Any damage that you take will be more likely to remain as recoverable health.
- The amount of recoverable health recovered upon hitting with or having an attack blocked will increase.
- Each character also gets their own individual power-ups, such as enhancements to the properties of certain techniques.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_745:
Heat Smash is a powerful technique that you can only use during Heat.

- Using it will consume all of the Heat Timer.
- The properties of the attack differ for each character.
- It can also destroy Hard Wall Breaks, Hard Floor Breaks, and other resilient stage breakables in a single attack.
- It cannot be defended against with attack reversals, parries, or Power Crushes.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_746: Heat Dash is an action that can be used in combos and offensive sequences. - Activate it by inputting (or {gui_bank0_heat}) until your Heat Engager connects with your opponent. - Using it will consume all of your Heat Timer. - Using Heat Dash will change some interactions with your opponent: Upon hit it will allow for follow-up attacks that cannot normally be performed, and even when blocked you will be able to move before your opponent. - When blocked, you will deal additional chip damage. - Any combo performed after landing a Heat Dash will leave the entirety of its damage as recoverable health. - An air combo that uses a Heat Burst will be more difficult to continue if you input a Heat Dash during it.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_747:
Heat provides the following properties.

- All attacks will deal chip damage.
- The amount of recoverable health regained upon hitting with or having an attack blocked will increase.
- When attacked while standing on the ground, the amount of recoverable health lost will be reduced.
- When attacked while airborne or downed, the amount of recoverable health that you receive will be increased.
- Each character also receives other enhancements to specific techniques and actions.
See the character Move Lists for more details.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_748:
A character cannot block attacks while they are in the air.
Hit with an attack that launches the opponent into the air and you can perform follow-up attacks until they return to the ground. This is called an air combo.

- Each attack that you land during an air combo will increase the distance you knock your opponent back. This makes it harder to continue landing attacks.
- You can use the Heat Dash to reduce the distance the opponent is knocked back.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_749:
Landing a specific attack on the opponent will put them in a tornado state.
Once tornado is triggered, the opponent will fall in a state that allows for easier follow-up attacks.

- Tornado can only be triggered once per combo.
- Some techniques can put the opponent into a tornado state from standing.
- You can also put the opponent into a tornado state after a successful low parry.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_750:
You can also perform combos by smashing the opponent into the walls placed around the stage. This is called a wall combo.
You can also perform a wall combo by smashing the opponent into the wall during an air combo.

- Wall combos pin your opponent to the wall, allowing for massive damage via follow-up attacks.
- Land an attack that triggers a tornado state during a wall combo and you can pin the opponent high up on the wall, making a further combo easier to land.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_751:
Some stages have walls with special destructible features.

- Wall Breaks/Balcony Breaks
These allow for follow-up attacks after breaking walls or barriers.
Upon performing the break, the opponent's recoverable health is also reduced.

- Hard Wall Breaks
Another type of wall break, triggered by performing two powerful hits while the opponent is on the wall.
A Heat Smash can perform the break in a single attack.

- Wall Blasts/Wall Bounds
Provides additional damage from a wall explosion.
The explosion also deals damage as recoverable health.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_752:
Some stages have special destructible sections of their floors.

- Floor Breaks
These allow for follow-up attacks after breaking through the floor.
Upon performing the break, the opponent's recoverable health is also reduced.

- Hard Floor Break
Another type of floor break, triggered by performing two powerful hits.
A Heat Smash can perform the break in a single attack.

- Floor Blast
Provides additional damage from a floor explosion.
The explosion also deals damage as recoverable health.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_753:
Each character's health is displayed via the Health Gauge at the top of the screen.

- Land attacks and reduce your opponent's health to zero to win by knockout.
- If the time runs out, the character with more health remaining will win.
Recoverable health is not included in the remaining health.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_754:
Recoverable health appears as part of the Health Gauge when damage is inflicted under certain conditions. Responding with certain actions will allow this type of health to be restored.

- Recoverable health occurs when you take damage while airborne or downed.
- Recoverable health can be restored by landing your own attacks or by having them blocked.
You cannot recover any health if your attack misses your opponent.
- When standing, being attacked by the opponent will gradually reduce your recoverable health.
- Certain other attacks will generate recoverable health when they hit or are blocked.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_755:
Blocking certain powerful techniques or attacks during Heat will deal damage that leaves recoverable health.
This damage is called chip damage.

- All chip damage is dealt as recoverable health.
- It is not possible to knock out an opponent using chip damage.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_756:
Once your health falls below a certain amount, you will enter a Rage state, increasing your attack power.

- During Rage, you can use a powerful attack called a Rage Art.
- You will take less chip damage during Rage.
- If damage that leaves recoverable health causes you to enter Rage, you will remain in Rage even if your health recovers above the required threshold.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_757:
During Rage, you have access to a powerful technique called a Rage Art.

- This is a special Power Crush that can absorb any attack, be it a high, mid, low, or throw.
- The less health you have left, the more damage it will do upon hit.
- Hitting with it will remove all of the opponent's recoverable health.
- It cannot be defended against with attack reversals, parries, or Power Crushes.
- Using it will end your Rage state.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_758:
A <term>Power Crush</> is a technique that allows you to absorb an opponent's attack while responding with one of your own.

- Any damage taken during a <term>Power Crush</> <highlight>will remain as recoverable health</>.
- If you have little health remaining, you may still be knocked out even if you absorb the incoming attack.
- Successfully absorbing an attack will enhance the properties of your own attack.
  *This includes increased damage, added chip damage, a smaller vulnerability window, and will beat an opponent's Power Crush or attack reversal.
- Power Crushes <highlight>cannot absorb low attacks and throws</>.
In the case of a throw, you will also not be able to perform a <highlight>throw escape</>.
- <term>Heat Bursts</>, <term>Heat Smashes</>, and <term>Rage Arts</> cannot be absorbed with a <term>Power Crush</>.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_759:
Throws are powerful attacks that cannot be blocked.

- Standard throws will only connect with standing opponents.
- Many throws are designed to catch sidestepping opponents more easily.
- After a throw connects, you can input a certain command to escape it called a throw escape.
- The commands for throw escapes differ depending on the throw, and can be viewed from the Move List.
- If a throw connects during a Power Crush or attack reversal, then you cannot perform a throw escape.
- Performing a counter hit throw will reduce the time allowed to input the throw escape.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_760: Crouching - Input , , or to put your character into a crouching state. - Blocking while crouching () will allow you to defend against low attacks. - While crouching, high attacks and standard throws will go over your head. (There are low throws that will only connect with a crouching opponent.) - Some techniques allow you to attack while maintaining the effects of crouching (crouching status). Rising - When crouching, input to stand back up. - Performing an attack before you have stood completely allows you to perform a rising attack.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_761: Airborne - Input , , or to make your character jump and become airborne. - You cannot block the opponent's attacks while airborne. - While airborne, you will evade low attacks and standard throws. (There are air throws that will only connect with an airborne opponent.) - Some techniques allow you to attack while maintaining the effects of being airborne, this is called jump status.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_762: Downed - The state of being knocked to the ground is called downed. - You are unable to block attacks when downed. - When downed, you can input or to stand up. - When downed, you can input to side roll and stand up. - When downed, you can input or to attack while getting up. Recovery - Just as you hit the ground, input or to perform a side quick roll. - You can evade attacks during a side ukemi.
TEXT_000_UI_PRACTICE_763: Sidestep / Side Walk - Input or to move your character into the background or foreground. This type of movement is called a sidestep. - Perform an attack during a sidestep to unleash an attack unique to your character. - A well-timed sidestep will allow you to evade linear attacks. - Input and hold or and you will continue to move in that direction. This type of movement is called side walking. Homing Attacks - Attacks that cannot be evaded by sidestepping or side walking.

TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_TUTORIAL_000: In Avatar Customization, you can change the appearance of your avatar in the lounge and Arcade Quest. There are all kinds of items, such as faces, hairstyles, and clothes, so feel free to create the look you like. You can always change your appearance later.
The slot screen and your Main Avatar
You can store up to 10 looks for your avatar. The avatar you use in the lounge is the one registered as your main avatar. You can switch your main avatar using the slot screen shown in this image.

You can use Fight Money to buy items for your avatar to equip, or get more by playing in-game modes like Arcade Quest. More items means more ways to express yourself. Get more items and find an outfit that suits you best.

By progressing through the game, you will be able to unlock new items for
your avatar. You can also use Fight Money or TC to purchase various items
via the Avatar Customization feature and the Tekken Shop.
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0000:
The Avatar Customization feature will be available once you've entered Arcade Quest or
Tekken Fight Lounge and created an avatar.
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0001:
There's already a customization in that slot.
Overwrite it?
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0002: Delete the customization data in this slot?
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0003:
This customized data contains items that you do not currently possess. These items will be removed if you edit this data.
Proceed anyway?
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0004: Your main avatar has been changed.
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0005:
Reset all the items and colors you're wearing
and go back to your previous appearance?
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0006:

If you wear it, the following items will be removed.
Continue anyway?
TEXT_000_UI_AVATARCUSTOMIZE_DIALOG_0007: Save this appearance and finish customizing your avatar?

Remove and re-equip it?

TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_000:
This is a multi-person battle against hordes of opponents.

Rout the incoming G Corp forces and try to secure the area.
Areas are secured and the Suppression Gauge is increased by defeating the required number of opponents.
Once an area is secured, combat for the current character will end.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_001:
Change targets with {gui_lt} or {btn_RS}.

{gui_lt}: Target closest opponent
{btn_RS}: Target closest opponent in entered direction.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_002:
When Heat is active, you can use Heat Smash multiple times with {gui_rb}.

Once Heat has ended, your Heat Gauge will recover over a set period of time, allowing you to activate Heat once again when it is full.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_003:
While in a Rage state, you can use Rage Burst once with {gui_rt}.

Rage Burst is a wide-range attack that knocks back surrounding foes.
The character's Rage state will end after using Rage Burst.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_004:
You can use sidestep ({gui_up} or {gui_down}) to avoid incoming attacks.
A successful dodge activates a short Heat state, letting you counter the attack with a Heat Smash.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_005:
The enemy forces include soldiers that can snipe you from long-range.

A laser sight (red line) will appear on you when you're being targeted.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_006:
Hold {gui_lt} to switch to Free Run mode.

During Free Run, you can use the directional buttons for 360-degree movement.
Useful for creating distance from the enemy, or closing the gap to faraway foes.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_IW_007:
Hold {gui_lt}: Free Run
{btn_RS}: Rotate Camera
{gui_lt} during Free Run: Target Lock-on

(Attack options are limited during Free Run.)
TEXT_000_UI_STORY_DIGEST_GUIDE_000:
Learn about the Mishima saga up until TEKKEN 7 with the digest movies.

Would you like to go to the Gallery before starting Story Mode?

The digest movies are recommended for first time players and
long time players who wish to refresh their memory.
TEXT_000_UI_ASSIST_STORY_000: Special Style has been enabled since the difficulty is set to Easy.
TEXT_000_UI_ASSIST_STORY_002:
You can try out Special Style in a battle.
Start the tutorial?
TEXT_000_UI_ASSIST_STORY_003:
During a battle, you will be able to perform special attacks after certain conditions have been met.
Start the Special Style tutorial for special moves?
TEXT_000_UI_TUTORIAL_STORY_001:
In Story Mode, you control a variety of characters as the story unfolds.
Use of the new Special Style function is recommended, allowing you to enjoy
exhilarating fights without having to memorize the controls.
TEXT_000_UI_TUTORIAL_STORY_002:
Would you like to view an explanation of Special Style?
(You can review this explanation later under the button settings in the Options menu.)
TEXT_000_UI_TUTORIAL_STORY_003:
The Special Style function lets you enjoy the action without memorizing each character's Move List.

For example, press △ repeatedly with Special Style turned on to make any character perform a recommended air combo.

(You can toggle Special Style on and off with the L1 button.)
(The buttons shown are based on the default button layout. They may differ depending on your controller settings.)
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_TITL: Enjoy battling with the new Special Style!
Special Style: Power Crush / Low Attack / Throw
Special Style: Heat Burst / Heat Smash
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_000: Use Tekken 8's new feature, "Special Style," to easily master your favorite characters' unique moves and combos! Even if you're new to using a character, you can enjoy fighting without having to memorize complicated controls right off the bat. Special Style can be toggled on or off with {gui_bank0_style} at any point during battle. Utilize Special Style while you're getting used to Tekken's controls!
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_001: Press {gui_bank0_LP} to unleash a Heat Engager ideal for offensive plays. If Heat is already activated, you'll use Heat Dash automatically. Just press {gui_bank0_LP} to enjoy TEKKEN 8's most exciting new feature: the Heat System. With Special Style, press {gui_bank0_LP} to start unleashing a flurry of attacks! Main Techniques can also be performed by pressing either {gui_bank0_LP}, {gui_bank0_LP}, or {gui_bank0_LP}!
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_002:
Press {gui_bank0_RP} for a combo starter.
After it hits, press {gui_bank0_RP} repeatedly to perform an air combo to deal massive
damage to your opponent. You'll perform different combos during Heat
and Rage, for even more damage.
Changing the buttons you press like {gui_bank0_RP}{gui_bank0_LP}{gui_bank0_LP}, {gui_bank0_RP}{gui_bank0_LK}{gui_bank0_LK} will allow you to
change the moves used in your combo.
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_002_:
Press {gui_bank0_RP} to perform a combo starter.
After the hit lands, repeatedly hit {gui_bank0_RP} to perform an air combo to inflict heavy damage on your opponent.
During Heat and Rage, combos will automatically change and damage will increase.
As seen with {gui_bank0_RP}{gui_bank0_LP}{gui_bank0_LP} and {gui_bank0_RP}{gui_bank0_LK}{gui_bank0_LK}, you can change the combo contents by changing the buttons pressed.
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_003: By pressing {gui_bank0_LK}, you can activate Power Crush, a counterattack used when getting attacked. Press {gui_bank0_LK} when you think the opponent is about to attack! Use {gui_bank0_RK} to perform low attacks, which are useful against Power Crushes and opponents who are blocking. When opponents become wary of your low attacks, hit them with special moves and air combos! Throws can also be performed with {gui_bank0_RK}, {gui_bank0_RK}, and {gui_bank0_RK}!
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_004:
{gui_bank0_heat} is a button that unleashes the Heat System's special moves.
Shift to Heat from a Heat Burst by pressing {gui_bank0_heat} before activating Heat.
Perform a Heat Smash by pressing {gui_bank0_heat} during Heat.
These moves can be incorporated into air combos.
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_004_:
Press {gui_bank0_heat} to perform Heat System's special move.
 You can press {gui_bank0_heat} before Heat activation to enter Heat with Heat Burst.
If you press {gui_bank0_heat} during Heat, Heat Smash can be activated.
These techniques can also be utilized in air combos.
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_HELPDIALOG_005:
You will go into a Rage state when your health is low.
This boosts your attack power, and you will be able to use your Rage Art with {gui_bank0_rage}.
Rage Arts work the same as Power Crushes, by absorbing blows and
dealing tons of damage to your opponent.
Once you enter a Rage state, press {gui_bank0_rage} to turn the tables on your opposition!
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_MSG_000:
Turn on Special Style?
– Can be toggled on or off during battle.
– Commands will return to normal when toggled off.
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_MSG_001:
As Special Style has not been assigned to any button, Special Style has been disabled.

To enable Special Style, adjust your control settings in Controller Setup.
TEXT_000_UI_SPECIALSTYLE_MSG_001_XB:
As Special Style has not been assigned to any button, Special Style has been disabled.

To enable Special Style, adjust your control settings in Controller Setup.
