TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_001:
You are free to explore the Arcade as you wish.
Approach other players to start up a conversation with them!
You can challenge them to a match or learn useful information.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_003:
Everyone here in the arcade loves the thrill of a fight!
Press {gui_detail} while talking to another player to challenge them to a match.
When you request a match, your conversation will stop and the battle will begin!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_005:
Rank can help you determine how strong another player is.
Win enough matches and you will be promoted.
Show everyone just how tough you are by reaching the higher ranks!
(Ranks change for each character separately.)
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_007:
Sometimes, a "!" will appear after defeating another player or getting promoted.
This mark means that you can continue to the next installment of the story.
Talk to a player with a "!" to continue the story.
The story will follow the highest-ranking character you are currently using.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_008: Take a trip to find opponents
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_009:
As the story progresses, there will be more arcades for you to visit.
Select Head Out to move to new arcades and challenge new fighters.

Talk to Max anytime after you've headed out to return to Gong.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_011:
The change in Rank Points is determined by the ranks of you and your opponent.
If you take on low-ranked opponents, winning won't earn you many Rank Points.
Check the icon to take on opponents similar to your own rank.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_013:
Take a look at the character profiles if you're not sure who to pick.
You can read the character settings as well as check their moves on video too.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_015:
Trying to learn all moves at once right from the get-go isn't the best way.
Open the Move List and press {gui_rb} to open the Main Techniques tab.
The Main Techniques tab lists the most important moves that you should learn first,
and also what the moves are good for!

If you don't understand something on the Move List,
check Command Help in Practice Mode.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_016: Use Special Style to fight even if you don't remember the commands
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_017:
Special Style is a function that lets you have fun fighting even if you don't know the commands.
With Special Style enabled, you can perform preselected moves repeatedly without any tricky button inputs.
You can enable Special Style by pressing {gui_bank0_style} during a battle.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_019:
If you send your opponent flying into a wall, you can put them in a defenseless state.
Adding follow-up attacks to that state is called a wall combo.
If you nail a wall combo, you can deal some huge damage!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_020: Smash into the wall from the front
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_021:
The trick to wall combos is to drive your opponent into the wall as straight as possible.
You can't make efficient follow-up attacks if your opponent hits the wall with their side.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_022: Change the angle so your opponent has their back to the wall
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_023:
Adjust the angle to land your wall combo just right.
Use Sidestep to position yourself so your opponent hits the wall straight on for best effect!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_025:
Hit an opponent close to a wall with attacks that knock them flying.
If the wall is weak, you can smash it, doing extra damage to your opponent!
This is called a Wall Break.
If you cause a Wall Break, you can perform more follow-up attacks.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_027:
Some fences in the stages are breakable.
If you perform a Wall Break close to one, you cause a Balcony Break.
Just like a Wall Break, you can hit your opponent with follow-up attacks before they can get up.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_029:
Some stages have walls that are rigged to explode.
Hit the spot hard enough and an explosion can deal extra damage!
That's called a Wall Blast.
You can also perform follow-up attacks on an opponent who's been knocked flying by a Wall Blast.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_031:
Some stages have places with flimsy floors.
You can perform a clever follow-up attack after an air combo to smash your opponent down.
Slam your opponent hard into the floor to cause a Floor Break!
You can also perform follow-up attacks on an opponent who has fallen to the floor below.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_033:
It's tough to land a combo on an opponent who uses a lot of Standing Blocks.
Try using low attacks, which aim for your opponent's legs,
or throws, which grabs your opponent and creates an opening in their defense!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_035:
If your opponent is making a flurry of attacks and it's hard to launch a combo starter,
try using a Power Crush, which doesn't get staggered even if you take a hit!
This lets you get your own attack off, even as you absorb your opponent's attacks.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_037: You can use to Sidestep to the foreground of the screen, and to Sidestep to the background. If you press or while doing a Sidestep, you will do a Side Walk. This move is important for both offense and defense, because you can avoid opponent attacks and escape when close to a wall.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_039: Try using Sidestep after attacks with small openings, like or . If your opponent blocks and then launches a straight-on attack like or , you can dodge it. Use the opening created by their missed attack to launch your own attack!
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_041:
You enter a Rage state when your health is low.
While in that state, your attacks do more damage, meaning you can make a comeback at the last moment.
Inflict some serious damage by landing an air combo while in a Rage state.
Using a Rage Art ends your Rage state, so be careful.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_043:
The damage from Rage Arts isn't fixed.
The less health you have left, the more damage your Rage Art does.
Launch your Rage Art at the right moment to deal as much damage as possible.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_045:
Similar to Power Crush, Rage Arts have properties that absorb attacks.
Power Crush can't absorb low attacks,
but Rage Arts can absorb high, medium, and low attacks.
But remember, if you keep absorbing damage until your health reaches zero, you'll be looking at a K.O.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_047:
When you land an air combo, it leaves a white area on your opponent's Health Gauge.
That's called the Recoverable Health Gauge.
Just like the name says, that part can be recovered during a battle.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_049:
The Recoverable Health Gauge recovers slightly every time you perform an attack.
Even if you take heavy damage from an air combo,
your health can still recover if you just hang in there and keep attacking.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_050: Power Crush and Recoverable Health Gauge
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_051:
If you absorb an attack with a Power Crush, you take a little damage,
but that damage remains as Recoverable Health Gauge.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_052: Chip Damage and Recoverable Health Gauge
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_053:
Some attacks are able to deal chip damage.
This means that even if your opponent blocks against them, you will still reduce their health a little, turning it into Recoverable Health Gauge.
If you're in Heat state, all of your attacks will inflict chip damage.
Chip damage cannot K.O. an opponent.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_054: Attack as your opponent gets up
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_055:
To do damage efficiently, the way you attack after an air combo is important.
An attack against a downed opponent is called a down attack.
Try to start a down attack right after your air combo ends.
The basis of a down attack is to use low attacks which let you attack close to the ground and have a low impact point.
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_056: Study the air combos that make down attacks easier
TEXT_000_UI_AQ_HELP_057:
Take care when choosing attacks that will serve as the finishing moves of your air combo.
If you end your air combo with a move that strikes the ground, it's easier to follow with a down attack.
Ideally, if the opponent has plenty of health left, use combos that emphasize down attacks,
but if you have a chance to K.O. them, then use combos with high total damage.

TEXT_000_UI_GAMEOPTIONS_119:
Tournament settings have been enabled, and the following changes to VS Battle have been implemented:

1) Controls
- During battle the {gui_menu} button will be disabled, preventing the game from being paused.
  Note: The game will not pause even if a controller is disconnected.

2) Rules
- All battles will be best of three.
- Each round will be 60 seconds.

3) Display/Sequences
- In this mode, the battle number and consecutive wins will not be displayed.
- Pre- and post-battle character sequences cannot be skipped.

TEXT_000_UI_ONLINEREPLAY_046:
Search for and watch the replays of matches played online.
Replays can only be saved for a limited amount of time, and will not appear in search results once they have expired.
Only matches with three rounds or less, each with sixty seconds or less can be saved as replays.
Replays can be saved for the following match types:
- Ranked Match
- Quick Match
- Player Match
- Group Match
(You must be connected to the network to use the Online Replay feature.)
TEXT_000_UI_ONLINEREPLAY_048:
You can watch any saved downloaded replays.
There is a limit to the number of replays that can be saved, and you will be unable to download any more once it is reached.
TEXT_000_UI_ONLINEREPLAY_050:
View the automatically-saved replays and tips of matches played online.
There is a limit to the number of replays that can be saved, and replays not added to favorites will be deleted from the oldest one first in order to save new ones.
Replays will be saved for the following match types:
- Ranked Match
- Quick Match
- Player Match
- Group Match
- Arcade Quest
- Super Ghost Battle
- VS Battle (excluding CPU vs CPU)
TEXT_000_UI_ONLINEREPLAY_052:
Watch a series of recommended replays chosen from online matches.
Replays will be chosen from the following match types:
- Ranked Match
- Quick Match
- Player Match
- Group Match
(You must be connected to the network to use the Exciting Replay feature.)

TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_072:
While watching a replay, the following tips will be shown to help you improve.
- Recommended Punishment Techniques
- Recommended Sample Combos
- High Moves You Can Duck Under
- Throw Escape Inputs

The tip display can be changed from the Pause Menu in the following three ways:
- Display while paused
- Display without pausing
- Hide
When set to Display while paused, you can
watch demos and practice recommended punishment techniques and sample combos.
TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_073:
Recommended Punishment Technique Tips will be displayed when you don't punish
one of your opponent's punishable common moves.
Press {gui_use} to watch a demo of the punishment technique while the tip is displayed.
You can also press {gui_decide} to practice the punishment technique for yourself.
Take advantage of opportunities to deal guaranteed damage whenever you can!
TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_074:
Recommended Sample Combo Tips will be displayed when you could have dealt more combo damage.
You can press {gui_use} to watch a demo of the combo while the tip is displayed.
You can also press {gui_decide} to practice the combo for yourself.
Take advantage of opportunities to deal guaranteed combo damage whenever you can.
TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_075:
High Moves You Can Duck Under Tips will be displayed when you
block an attack sequence that contains a move that can be punished by crouching.
There are many attacks that cannot be punished when blocked, so knowing when to
crouch will give you a huge advantage and the chance to strike back.
TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_076:
Throw Escape Tips will be displayed when you are unable to escape a throw.
Escape throws by pressing the correct command immediately after being grabbed.
TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_077:
Have you ever wondered what would've happened if you had used a different move?
Scratched your head over how to respond to an opponent's attacks?
Or, if you'd just dealt a bit more combo damage, would it have been a K.O.?

Well, now you can take control of your character and find out for yourself
by pressing {gui_lsb} or {gui_rsb}.
TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_078:
After enabling character control, an icon will indicate who you can use.
The replay will continue playing until you input a command.
TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_079:
Input a command when you want to perform an action different from the replay.
The icon will change color to indicate that you can control the character,
so you can test out moves that you weren't able to do during the match.

TEXT_000_UI_MYREPLAY_083: Learn about how to control a character during a replay.

TEXT_000_UI_TEKKENBALL_INFO_002: A beach ball that feels familiar somehow.
TEXT_000_UI_TEKKENBALL_INFO_003: An iron ball that requires an iron fist to play with.
TEXT_000_UI_TEKKENBALL_INFO_004: A commemorative monument holding a G Corp Ball was erected in New York City to celebrate G Corp's achievements.
TEXT_000_UI_TEKKENBALL_INFO_005: A decoration that can be found at the Battle Area entrance in the Tekken Fight Lounge. Who brought it there?
TEXT_000_UI_TEKKENBALL_INFO_006: A ball created by condensing negative human emotion into solid matter. It gives off an ominous miasma. (Changes some sound effects.)
TEXT_000_UI_TEKKENBALL_INFO_007: A ball created to make fun of the world-spanning and intergenerational Mishima family conflict. (Sets off fireworks after a K.O.)
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TITLE_TEKKENBALL_: Use Devilish effects to get the upper hand!
Toss the ball for powerful Devilish effects!
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_01_001:
In Tekken Ball, hit the ball into your opponent's side of the court—or the
opponent themselves—to deal damage to them. Reduce your opponent's health
to zero to win! You cannot deal damage to your opponent by directly attacking
them, so keep your eyes on the ball.
(The behavior of some moves like throws and Rage Arts will differ in this mode.)
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_01_002:
Hit the ball with a strong move, and it'll go flying toward your opponent as an
Attack Ball. Your opponent will take damage if they are hit. You can deflect
an Attack Ball by hitting it with a move of your own or blocking against it.
Be careful, though, as you will take a small amount of damage when blocking.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_01_003:
The Power Gauge shows how much damage an Attack Ball will do.
Power will build up every time the ball gets hit by a move.
The fuller the Power Gauge, the more damage dealt when it hits a player.
The Power Gauge persists until the ball hits a player or lands on the court.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_01_004:
Hit the ball with certain moves to give it a Devilish effect.
Use a Power Crush for a Fast Ball, a homing move for a Zigzag Ball, and a Tornado move for a Drop Ball.
Heat Smashes and Rage Arts will activate one of the three Devilish effects at random, making it extremely difficult for your opponent to deflect.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_01_005:
You can Toss the ball by performing a throw on an opponent's Attack Ball.
Hitting a tossed ball with a move will give it a quick and powerful Devilish
effect at random.
It's a bit of a technical move, so give it a try once you've become comfortable
with the basics.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_01_006:
It is possible to slow the ball down by canceling a Heat Burst, allowing you to return the ball safely or hit it with a powerful move of your own.
Hitting the ball with a Heat Engager and performing a Heat Dash will also slow it down, giving you the chance to smash it with a powerful move for huge damage.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_02_001:
Landing a low, mid, or high attack on a ball turns it into an Attack Ball.
However, weak high or low attacks will not result in an Attack Ball.
Also, hitting the ball with an unblockable attack while the Power Gauge has no
charge will not inflict damage on the opponent.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_02_002:
Hit the ball with certain moves to give it a Devilish effect.
Use a Power Crush for a Fast Ball, a homing move for a Zigzag Ball,
and a Tornado move for a Tornado Ball. Heat Smashes and Rage Arts will activate
a random Devilish effect, making it extremely hard for your opponent to deflect.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_02_003: You can Toss the ball by performing a throw with inputs like or on an Attack Ball. Hitting a tossed ball with a move will give it a random, powerful Devilish effect, boosting the ball's speed and damage. It's a bit of a technical move, so give it a try once you've become comfortable with the basics first.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_02_004:
Grounding the ball on the opponent's side of the court will inflict damage on
them. There is, however, a Safe Area that extends two meters from either side
of the center line. No damage is dealt when the ball falls into this zone.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_02_005: Just like in battle, you can back dash with . Press to perform a long back dash to cover more distance. Use to retreat quickly—perfect for when the ball goes long.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_02_006:
It is possible to slow the ball down by canceling a Heat Burst,
letting you return the ball safely or hit it with a powerful move of your own.
Hitting the ball with a Heat Engager and performing a Heat Dash will also slow
it down, giving you the chance to smash it with a powerful move for huge damage.
TEXT_000_UI_HELPDIALOG_TEKKENBALL_02_007:
When you're in a tight spot, your Health Gauge and character will glow red,
putting you in a Rage state. This will boost your attack strength and lets you
activate Rage Burst with {gui_bank0_rage}. It boasts a wide attack range and triggers a random
Devilish effect when it hits the ball. The high attack power of this move can be
used for an easy comeback, but it takes you out of Rage, so use with caution.

TEXT_000_UI_PLAYERCUSTOMIZE_TUTORIAL_000: In Player Customization, you can customize the information and certain UI design elements displayed during and before battles. You can also restrict character movement following a K.O. in the battle. Please note that some elements will take effect on your screen only, and will not be visible to your opponent.

Choose your title and the design of the plate behind it. These are displayed before a battle begins and also on the battle screen.
Find a title and plate that show your opponent what you're all about.

Change the design of the Health Gauges displayed on the battle screen.
Your customization will affect both you and your opponent's Health Gauge, but it will not be visible to your opponent.

Change the design of your player information panel displayed on the battle screen.
This customization will only affect your panel, and your opponent can customize
their own.

Setting Magic Mirror will limit character movement after a battle's winner is decided.
Please note that this will not affect what your opponent or any match spectators see.

TEXT_000_UI_CHARACTERCUSTOMIZE_TUTORIAL_: In Character Customization, you can change the appearance of the characters you use in battles. There are many items besides hairstyles and clothes, so have fun creating unique looks for your favorite characters. You can use Fight Money to buy items, or unlock them by playing various in-game modes.

Some accessories let you change their size, position, color, and more. They can look totally different, depending on how you use them. There are some accessories which will take up all accessory slots. If you equip two items that can perform actions in battle, please take note of the activation commands and make sure they do not overlap.

You can upload character appearances you've created to the Customization Market. Select "Upload" on the Character Select screen to upload up to 10 appearances. Other players can freely download and use what you've created, but they won't be able to change the content of those appearances.

You can check character appearances you've downloaded from other players in the Customization Market with "Manage Downloaded Appearances" on the Character Select screen and up to 20 appearances can be saved. You can go to the Customization Market from the Customization Area of the lounge.

In Character Customization, you use items like clothes and accessories to change your character's appearance in all kinds of ways. Many players will see the items you created in online battles and elsewhere. Note that if you create something that is deemed offensive or violates the law, you may be penalized, preventing you from playing the game.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_000: An archaeologist discovered a cave painting. It depicted...
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_001: Six months after Heihachi Mishima's disappearance, Jin headed to central New York to defeat Kazuya.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_002: One week after the battle with Kazuya, as Jin was heading into battle once more, a deadly change was happening in his body...
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_003: Jin competed in the area preliminaries, but he was far from being in the best condition. Just then, an unknown girl appeared before him.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_004: Heading for Italy, they were joined by an expert who had requested an investigation into the devil gene.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_005: The finals of The King of Iron Fist Tournament had at last begun, setting off a fierce struggle between the winners of the area preliminaries.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_006: The rebel army burst into the arena and succeeded in interrupting the tournament, but Kazuya's plan was already in its final stage.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_007: The group stood together to confront and defeat the newly empowered Kazuya.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_008: In order to retrace his origins, Jin and his group headed for Yakushima. They arrived at the foot of a giant cedar tree that holds memories of his childhood.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_009: Xiaoyu looks after Jin, who lost consciousness. Meanwhile, a follow-up attack by G Corp was closing in fast.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_010: The rebel army gathered all its strength to confront the horde of G Corp soldiers deployed to catch Jin.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_011: Xiaoyu fought desperately to protect Jin from the G Corp assault, but she was nearing her limit...
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_012: A mysterious space spreads out before Jin's eyes when he wakes up from his clouded consciousness. What will he see there?
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_013: Having fully accepted himself and regained his strength, Jin challenges Kazuya to a final battle.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_014: The "two stars" battle furiously atop an enormous volcanic rock as it plummets to the ground, in a truly mythical conflict.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_015: The conflict between Jin and Kazuya was nearing its epic conclusion at last.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_016: A fate bound by blood is eternal...
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_MAIN_TASK_002: Defeat a certain number of opponents
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_000: One day, a friend takes you with them to the arcade to check out the latest TEKKEN game. It's the very first tournament of its kind you've ever seen, but immediately, you're struck by how the atmosphere is bursting at the seams with the players' enthusiasm and competitive spirit.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_001: With the help of your friends, you get a grasp of the basics of the game, and slowly, you grow more skilled over time. To see how much you've grown, you decide to hold a small tournament with your friends. Not only is it your first tournament, but it's also your first chance to really show off your skills.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_002: However, you and your friends are ridiculed as "wannabe fighters" by the players you had so admired. To prove your skills and get revenge, you decide to go set out for other arcades. First up is a place where tons of players who want to casually enjoy TEKKEN gather to play.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_003: Next, you learn of a new arcade. You hear players who are serious about customization favor that spot. So, in order to further hone your abilities, you decide to head there as well.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_004: Arcade after arcade, you continue your quest in search of ever more powerful opponents. At long last, you arrive at a mysterious arcade that seems emerge out of the darkness. You sense that new and even more fierce competitors await you inside.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_005: This place is a holy land for players who truly love TEKKEN to gather. If you win the tournament held here, you will earn the right to continue on to an even bigger, even grander tournament. The time has come for you to showcase the skills you have fought to win over your long journey.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_006: What lays before you is the tournament of your dreams. In order to prove your worth as TEKKEN fighters, it is time for you to finally face your sworn enemy.
TEXT_000_UI_ACTIVITY_AQ_007: As you celebrate your victory, you hear of a new, even more ferocious competitor from overseas. Truly, nothing whets your appetite like the prospect of a formidable opponent.

TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_001:
PS5® Tournaments are official game tournaments held by PlayStation® that you can participate in. There are chances for grand prizes depending on your tournament results.

A subscription to PlayStation®Plus is needed to join the tournament. Preconditions may include age, county/region, etc. For more details, check "Tournament Rules."
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_002: Registering is necessary to play in a tournament. Players must enter the lobby once it becomes time for the tournament. The match will start as soon as both opponents are present. Win the match to proceed to the next round.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_003: From the Main Menu, select Online > PS5® Tournaments > Enter Tournament to view a list of tournaments that match your conditions. Select a tournament you wish to participate in to access the Tournament Activity Card.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_004: Access "Upcoming Tournaments" in the Game Hub to view a list of thumbnails of tournaments that match your conditions. Select a thumbnail of a tournament you wish to participate in to access the Tournament Activity Card.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_005: Access "Tournaments" from the Control Center to view a list of tournaments that match your conditions. Select a tournament you wish to participate in to access the Tournament Activity Card.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_006: You can invite Friends to tournaments and also accept tournament invites sent from your Friends. To invite a Friend, select "Invite" from the overflow menu (...) accessible on the Tournament Activity Card and select the Friend that you would like to invite. When you receive an invite, select "See More Details" to access the Tournament Activity Card.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_007: The Tournament Activity Card is used to register for, participate in, and check information related to tournaments. The Tournament Activity Card can be accessed via the Main Menu, Game Hub, and Control Center. The information and actions available will change depending on the tournament status.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_008:
Tournament Activity Card before Registering for Tournaments

Register for tournaments and check the starting time and date, as well as rewards. Invite other players and check the Tournament Rules from the overflow menu (...). Select "Show Details" to check for more detailed tournament information.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_009:
Tournament Activity Card after Registering for Tournaments

"Join Now" will be shown after registering for a tournament, but this cannot be selected until the start of the tournament. Cancel registration, invite other players, and check the Tournament Rules from the overflow menu (...). Use the down button to scroll along the Tournament Activity Card to check for more detailed tournament information.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_010:
Tournament Activity Card after Tournaments Start

"Join Now" can be selected once the tournament has started. Withdraw from tournaments, invite players, and check the Tournament Rules from the overflow menu (...). Select "Tournament Bracket" to view results of the tournament you are participating in. Use the down button to scroll along the Tournament Activity Card to check for more detailed tournament information.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_011:
Tournament Activity Card in the Lobby

"Resume" will be shown when you are in the lobby. Nothing will happen if you select "Resume" while you are in the lobby. Select "Show Details" to check for more detailed tournament information.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_012:
Tournament Activity Card after Tournaments End

Check your rank and results of your last battle. Select "Show Details" to check for more detailed tournament information.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_013: Tournament Rules will be shown after accessing the Tournament Activity Card and selecting "Register." Read the Tournament Rules and select "Accept" to register to the tournament. There is no limit on the number of tournaments you can register to.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_014: After registering for a tournament, you have up until the time of the tournament to select "Cancel" from the overflow menu (...). It is accessible from the Tournament Activity Card to cancel registration to a tournament.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_015: The Tournament Rules can be checked when you register for a tournament. After registering, the rules can be checked from the overflow menu (...) accessible from the Tournament Activity Card.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_016: Access the Tournament Activity Card when it is time for the tournament match and select "Join Now" to enter a lobby where you will wait for your opponent.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_017: Perform Quick Select after going to the lobby and select your character, CPU's character, customization slot, play side and stage. The options chosen here will only affect matches played in the lobby.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_018:
You can practice in a mode similar to "Practice" while waiting for your opponent in the lobby. Your current status will be displayed while you are waiting. This status will change based on the matching conditions. 

Press the options button in the lobby to view the Lobby Menu. This menu also has a mode similar to "Practice", and from here, you can change various settings, practice, and more.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_019: Leaving the lobby will result in removal of your participation status. If you wish to proceed in the tournament, access the Tournament Activity Card and select "Join Now" within the time limit.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_020: When you and your opponent are both present in the lobby, the match will begin once you are paired successfully.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_021: Notifications will be sent out even from Round 2 onwards. Just like for Round 1 of the tournament, players will have to go to the lobby within 5 minutes of the message being sent out, or the player will not be able to join the match.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_022: If you do not go to the lobby within 5 minutes of the tournament start notification, you will not be able to join the tournament. The match will count as a loss for players who do not join the tournament in time. If both matched-up players do not join the tournament, a winner will be decided at random. However, note that this does not apply to the Final Match and that both players will end up in second place if this happens.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_023: When you are not in the lobby, open the Tournament Activity Card and select "Join Now" or "Resume" to return to the lobby and rejoin the tournament.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_024: You can select "Withdraw" from the overflow menu (...) accessible from the Tournament Activity Card when the tournament begins. Withdrawing will immediately result in a tournament loss.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_001: Rewards vary depending on how you perform in a tournament. Check the rewards via the Tournament Activity Card.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_002: When you go to the lobby, a window will appear announcing the start of the tournament. Use this opportunity to confirm the rules of the match.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_003: Select "Tournament Bracket" from the Tournament Activity Card to see the overall tournament results as well as individual players' results.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_004: When you go to the lobby, any game you are currently playing will be ended, and any progress made will not be saved.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_005: This is the notification you will receive 15 minutes prior to the start of the tournament.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_006:
This is the notification that will arrive once the tournament starts. Open your Tournament Activity Card, then select "Join Now." Once you do, you will go to the lobby and wait for your opponent.

There is a limit for how much time you can take before going to the lobby. Matching with your opponent can take a while as well, so be sure to leave yourself a bit of extra time and head to the lobby early.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_007: If you win a match, you will receive a win notification and can continue on to the next round. Once preparations for the next round are complete, you will receive a new notification for the start of the tournament.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_008: If you lose a match, you will receive a loss notification and be eliminated from the tournament.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_009: If you win in the final round, you will receive a notification indicating that you have won the tournament.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_010: Your rank will ultimately be determined by which matches you lost and whether or not you won the final round. You can check the rank you reached by accessing the Tournament Activity Card.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_011: If the number of players signed up for a tournament does not meet the tournament's minimum requirement, then the tournament will be canceled, and you will receive a notification.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_012: If the tournament's organizer cancels the tournament, you will receive a message notifying you of its cancelation.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_013: If another tournament you registered for begins while you are still in the middle of a different one, then your registration for the tournament you are not currently participating in will be automatically canceled.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_014: The game disc is necessary in order to participate in a tournament with the physical version of the game. If the game disc is not inserted, please follow the directions on screen and insert the game disc.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_015: If your opponent fails to show up for the match, then you will receive a notification that you have won by default once you are in the lobby. This can count as a win for a match in a tournament.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_016: You can view the total number of tournaments you have participated in as well as the number of times you have achieved a high rank by visiting "Tournaments" in the Game Hub.
TEXT_000_Tournament_Tutorial_Text02_017: If another tournament you registered for begins while you are still in the middle of a different one, then your registration for the tournament you are not currently participating in will be automatically canceled, and you will receive a notification.

TEXT_001_UI_BP_DIALOG_LARGE_TEXT_001:
– Premium Pass and Premium Pass Plus upgrades can only be used for the round in which they were purchased.
– The rewards which can be earned through the Premium Pass and Premium Pass Plus are the same.
– Once your Fight Pass reaches Lv. 41, you cannot purchase the Premium Pass Plus.
– Once you have purchased the Premium Pass, you cannot purchase the Premium Pass Plus.
– The duration of the Fight Pass and the sales period for the Premium Pass and Premium Pass Plus are subject to change without notice.
– Due to the nature of this product, refunds or cancellations cannot be provided.

TEXT_001_UI_BP_REWARDDIALOG_MSG_001: You earned the following rewards for leveling up:
TEXT_001_UI_BP_HELPDIALOG_TEXT_001: The Tekken Fight Pass feature offers a variety of rewards for playing the game. For instance, playing online will help you increase your Fight Pass's level, earning you corresponding rewards the higher the level you reach. With the purchase of a Premium Pass, you can earn even more rewards.
TEXT_001_UI_BP_HELPDIALOG_TEXT_002: By upgrading your Tekken Fight Pass to a Premium Pass, you will earn rewards that are exclusive to the Premium Pass on top of your original Fight Pass rewards. With Premium Pass Plus, you can enjoy not only the benefits of a Premium Pass, but a boost to your Fight Pass's level as well.
TEXT_001_UI_BP_HELPDIALOG_TEXT_003: Your Fight Pass's level can be increased by building up Fight Pass EXP. Fight Pass EXP is earned by playing in certain Online Modes and completing missions. In addition, when you upgrade to a Premium Pass, you can directly purchase levels for your Fight Pass.
TEXT_001_UI_BP_HELPDIALOG_TEXT_004: You can also efficiently increase your Fight Pass level by completing a wide array of missions and earning Fight Pass EXP. Visit the Missions page to see a list of missions which are available for you to take on right away. Reap the benefits and earn EXP.

TEXT_002_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_025:
Once a tournament has started and you select "Join Now," leaving the lobby will result in you being removed from the tournament.
This includes the following situations:
- Accepting another invitation
- Returning to the Main Menu from the Lobby Menu
TEXT_002_Tournament_Tutorial_Text01_026: If an error occurs after joining the lobby and you are returned to the Main Menu, open the Tournament Activity Card and select "Resume" to return to the lobby and rejoin the tournament.

TEXT_VO_003_dog_sta_00_000: I swear on my master's name, I will not lose.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_sta_01_000: Keep a passionate heart and a cool head.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_sta_02_000: I'll show you capoeira at its finest.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_sta_00_vr00_000: There's nothing for you to be sorry about.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_sta_00_vr01_000: I'll bring you to your senses soon enough.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_sta_02_vr00_000: This isn't about revenge—it's about a brighter future.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_win_00_000: No one can lay a hand on me.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_win_02_000:
You may go down today, but you'll get another chance...
Just like me.

TEXT_VO_003_dog_sta_01_vr00_000: Your footwork could use some improvement.
TEXT_VO_003_dog_win_02_vr02_000: You've got some good kicks. But I know you can do better.

TEXT_005_UI_PRACTICE_771:
Use attack reversals and parries with impeccable timing to render your opponent's attacks ineffective.

These two techniques can be used to block standard punch and kick attacks from your opponents.
Strong attack reversals and parries can be used to block elbow smashes, knee hits, airborne attacks, weapon attacks, and more.
Complete evasions offer even more protection, allowing you to dodge moves with the reversal break effect like:
- Heat Smashes
- Unblockable attacks
- Certain character-unique techniques
- Max-level hold attacks

Check the Move List to see which moves have strong attack reversal, strong parry, and complete evasion properties.
TEXT_005_UI_PRACTICE_772:
A Power Crush is a technique that allows you to absorb an opponent's attack while responding with one of your own.

- Any damage taken during a Power Crush will remain as recoverable health.
- If you have little health remaining, you may still be knocked out even if you absorb the incoming attack.
- Successfully absorbing an attack will enhance the properties of your own attack.
*This includes added chip damage, a smaller vulnerability window, and will beat an opponent's Power Crush or attack reversal.
- Power Crushes cannot absorb low attacks and throws.
In the case of a throw, you will also not be able to perform a throw escape.
- Moves with the reversal break effect cannot be absorbed with a Power Crush.

TEXT_005_UI_PRACTICE_773: Heat Burst is a technique that allows you to activate Heat whenever you like. - Input the command and you will activate Heat and perform an attack at the same time. - It has the same properties as a Power Crush and can absorb high or mid attacks. - It will also consume some of your Heat Timer. - You can press to cancel the attack part. - Any combo performed after landing a Heat Burst will leave the entirety of its damage as recoverable health.
TEXT_005_UI_PRACTICE_774: Heat Dash is an action that can be used in combos and offensive sequences. - Activate it by inputting (or {gui_bank0_heat}) until your Heat Engager connects with your opponent. - Using it will consume all of your Heat Timer. - You will also regain some of your recoverable health. - Using Heat Dash will change some interactions with your opponent. - Upon hit, it will allow for follow-up attacks that cannot normally be performed, and even when blocked, you will be able to move before your opponent. - When blocked, you will deal additional chip damage. - Any combo performed after landing a Heat Dash will leave the entirety of its damage as recoverable health. - An air combo that uses a Heat Burst will be more difficult to continue if you input a Heat Dash during it.
TEXT_005_UI_PRACTICE_775:
Throws are powerful attacks that cannot be blocked.

- Standard throws will only connect with standing opponents.
- After a throw connects, you can input a certain command to escape it called a throw escape.
- The commands for throw escapes differ depending on the throw, and can be viewed from the Move List.
- If a throw connects during a Power Crush or attack reversal, then you cannot perform a throw escape.
- Performing a counter hit throw will reduce the time allowed to input the throw escape.

TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_233:
In Photo Mode, you can enjoy taking pictures of your favorite characters mid-battle from a wide variety of angles.
Use different camera lenses, effects, and filters to fine-tune a shot until you've created the perfect photo!
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_235: In Camera Settings, you can change the lens type as well as choose which elements to show or hide, such as characters, HUD, control guide, effects, and more.
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_237:
With Depth of Field, adjust various settings like autofocus, depth of field, zoom, and blur level.
In Zoom, adjust the background focus level by changing the photo's scope and blur level.
- A lower depth of field level will give shallower background clarity, whereas a higher level will give deeper background clarity.
- A lower zoom level will zoom in the camera for closeups, whereas a higher level will zoom out for a wider shot.
- A lower blur level will decrease blur, whereas a higher level will increase it.
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_239:
In Screen Effects 1, apply and adjust effects like lens flare, bloom, chromatic aberration, dirt mask, and more.
- A higher bloom level will create a stronger "glowing" effect.
- A higher chromatic aberration will intensify the color distortion effect.
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_241:
In Screen Effects 2, apply and adjust effects like vignette, white balance, saturation, contrast, brightness, and more.
- A lower vignette level will brighten the corners of the screen, whereas a higher level will darken them.
- A lower contrast level will reduce the difference between light and dark areas on the screen, giving a blurred effect. A higher contrast level will make the difference between light and dark more pronounced.
- A lower brightness level will make the screen darker, whereas a higher level will make it brighter.
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_243: In Filter Settings 1, apply and adjust the type, effect, intensity, noise, and other aspects of color filters.
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_245: In Filter Settings 2, apply and adjust the type, effect, and other aspects of water drop filters.
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_278: Adjust the water drop filter's UV scaling.
TEXT_006_UI_PHOTO_279: Select the water drop filter type.

TEXT_007_UI_ONLINEREPLAY_002:
View the automatically saved replays and tips of matches played online.
There is a limit to the number of replays that can be saved, and replays not added to favorites will be deleted from the oldest one first in order to save new ones.
Replays will be saved for the following match types:
- Ranked Match / Quick Match
- Player Match / Group Match
- Arcade Quest / Super Ghost Battle
- VS Battle (excluding CPU vs CPU)
- Tekken Ball (no tips)
TEXT_007_UI_ONLINEREPLAY_003:
View the replays of Tekken Ball matches played online.
Replays can only be saved for a limited amount of time and will not appear in search results once they have expired.
Tips will not be available in Tekken Ball replays.
(You must be connected to the network to use the Tekken Ball Replays feature.)

TEXT_007_UI_PRACTICE_779: Heat Burst is a technique that allows you to activate Heat whenever you like. - Input the command and you will activate Heat and perform an attack at the same time. - It has the same properties as a Power Crush and can absorb high or mid attacks. - It will also consume some of your Heat Timer. - You can press to cancel the attack part. - Heat Burst damage cannot K.O. - Any combo performed after landing a Heat Burst will leave the entirety of its damage as recoverable health.
TEXT_007_UI_PRACTICE_780:
Recoverable health appears as part of the Health Gauge when damage is inflicted under certain conditions.
Responding with certain actions will allow this type of health to be restored.

- Some damage taken while airborne or downed will remain as recoverable health.
Any damage taken during Heat will remain as recoverable health.
- When standing, being hit by the opponent's attacks will reduce some of your recoverable health.
During Heat, your recoverable health will not decrease.
- Recoverable health can be restored by landing your own attacks or by having them blocked.
The amount of recoverable health recovered with each hit increases in relation to attack damage, however there is a limit.
You cannot recover any health if your attack misses your opponent.
During Heat, the amount of recoverable health recovered with each hit and recoverable health limit increase, making it easier to recover health.
- Certain other attacks will generate recoverable health when they hit or are blocked.
TEXT_007_UI_PRACTICE_781:
During Rage, you have access to a powerful technique called a Rage Art.

- This is a special Power Crush that can absorb any attack, be it a high, mid, low, or throw.
- The less health you have left, the more damage it will do upon hit.
- You will also regain some of your recoverable health.
- Landing a Rage Art will remove all the opponent's recoverable health.
- It cannot be defended against with attack reversals, parries, or Power Crushes.
- Using it will end your Rage state.

TEXT_008_UI_DLC_STORY_CHAPTER_TITLE_008: The King of the Iron Fist Returns
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_001: The battle between the Mishima Zaibatsu, led by Heihachi, and G Corp, led by Kazuya, came to a close with G Corp claiming victory—leaving the world to be ruled by an iron fist. In order to turn the situation around, Lars takes the rebel army and initiates "Operation Lightning" with his ace in the hole: Jin Kazama and his devil power. However, Jin is defeated by Devil Kazuya, who senses the weakness within him. Kazuya then declares to the world at large that he will hold a tournament to decide the fate of each country.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_002: Jin is rescued from beneath the rubble by Alisa following his utter defeat at the hands of Kazuya. He would then spend one whole week to heal before he was even able to move. In order to assess Jin's abilities, Lars spars with him only to realize that Jin's abilities have taken a huge decline—to the point he was almost like another person—and that he no longer possesses the ability to transform into a devil. Jin believes that he can regain his devil power if he engages in a high-stakes deadly battle. Thus, he decides to participate in the tournament that Kazuya is holding.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_003: Although nowhere near top-fighting form, Jin is able to advance to the semifinals. His next opponent is a mysterious girl by the name of Reina who calls him "Jin Senpai." Jin struggles against the girl, who uses a form of Mishima-Style Fighting Karate. During the fight, he almost loses control and unleashes his devil power, securing him the win. While fighting with Hwoarang in the finals, he remembers the thrill of battle and takes the reins of his devil power to claim victory.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_004: After Xiaoyu learns that Jin is safe and sound, she ventures out to the final venue of the tournament, the Colosseum, with Claudio and Zafina. Jin and the others, also on their way to Italy, meet up with Niklas and Leo. The group find out about how the devils were created to be a servant to the original devil, Azazel, and how the devils' power originates from the emotion of desire. Meanwhile, Victor and his UN troops call for an alliance with the rebel army to put an end to Kazuya's rampage.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_005: The final leg of the tournament has commenced. While intense battles that rile up the crowd unfold, the UN and rebel army arrive and deploy their troops. Just when Jin rises as the victor over Leroy, Kazuya appears in the arena and further fans the flames of conflict in people's hearts. Jin sees his past self in Kazuya, prompting Leroy to ask, "Who are you?" Reflecting on those words, Jin questions who he really is...and once again comes to the realization of what he must do.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_006: The UN troops and the rebel army prove successful in infiltrating the Colosseum and stopping the tournament from proceeding any further. However, Kazuya grabs hold of Zafina, and he proceeds to use the negative emotions concentrated in the arena to resurrect the original demon, Azazel, who was sealed away within Zafina's arm. Azazel believes Kazuya to be its servant and tries to absorb him into itself. However, it is Kazuya who absorbs the ancient being instead, awakening a power more sinister than ever before...
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_007: Before Zafina falls unconscious, she speaks a final premonition. Heeding her words, Jin heads to his birthplace, Yakushima, with the help of his allies. Lars and the others remain behind and stand against Kazuya to try to buy time for Jin. However, they are faced with the bitter truth that they cannot hold a candle to Kazuya's power. Forced into a dire situation, while Reina is engaged in battle with Kazuya, Claudio summons all his might in a single arrow aimed at him as well, which hits its mark to great effect. Unable to control Azazel's power, Kazuya retreats, and so Lars and the others narrowly escape death's grasp.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_008: Jin lands in his hometown of Yakushima and, along with Xiaoyu, starts heading to the place where he was born and raised. He then proceeds to spar with Xiaoyu in front of the grand Yakusugi tree where he used to train with his mother. Following that, Jin hears a Kazama nursery rhyme that was sung to him when he was a child. He follows the source of it and finds himself being lured in to the Yakusugi tree shrine. He promises Xiaoyu that he will return, and then loses consciousness.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_009: The rebel army have a briefing to plan out the final battle in order to save Jin from the G Corp forces that are invading Yakushima. And although they were vastly outnumbered by G Corp, a number of tournament participants decided to fight alongside them. However, the first of the G Corp troops have already reached Yakushima. Xiaoyu takes on a seemingly endless number of Jack-7s with a strong determination in her heart to protect the one she holds dear. An unconscious Jin begins a traversal into his own psyche.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_010: The UN and rebel army troops on Yakushima engage in a full-on battle with G Corp, who are after Jin. Lars and the others are putting up a valiant effort, taking down G Corp soldiers when a Jack-8, the newest model of eradication weapons, is sent onto the battlefield. Alisa and the others succeed in stopping Jack-8 in his tracks. The situation looks to be under control, and the end in sight until Kazuya flies over Yakushima. He tears through the battlefield, indiscriminately laying waste to friend or foe, and then heads to the grand Yakusugi tree to settle things with Jin for good.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_011: As Xiaoyu fends off the Jack-7s to protect Jin, she finds herself slowly losing the upper hand. Lars joins the fray in the nick of time and, together, they succeed in taking down the Jack-7s. However, it would prove to be too early to celebrate. Kazuya arrives on the scene looking for Jin. Lars decides to face Kazuya by himself. Despite the massive difference in power, Lars continues to fight with the belief that Jin will awaken soon.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_012: As Jin explores the depths of his mind, he is faced with the devil that resides within him. First, it takes the form of himself, consumed by revenge. Second, it takes the form of him as the head of the Mishima clan. Lastly, it takes the form of the power needed to protect himself... As he fights with each form of the devil, Jin realizes that it has been protecting him this whole time. He then proceeds to break the chains that he had put on his heart, and accepts the devil inside of him, thus retrieving his power.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_013: Jin regains his powers and rescues Lars, who is on the verge of death. He then proceeds to have a few last words with Xiaoyu before proceeding to take on Kazuya. During their intense battle, Kazuya unleashes a colossal mass of energy at Jin. Jin puts his life on the line to stop this attack, and in the process has a spiritual reencounter with his supposedly dead mother, Jun Kazama. After meeting his mother, he awakens his power of purification and gains the upper hand. However, shortly after, he and Kazuya are both caught in an underwater volcano eruption that hurtles them both into the stratosphere.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_014: As the volcanic rock plummets back to Earth, Jin and Kazuya engage in a battle so furious that it transcends all human comprehension. The rock gradually breaks apart, causing the two to fall faster by the second. They both unleash a final blow of energy so enormous that it causes their devil powers to disappear completely.
TEXT_008_UI_MAIN_STORY_SUMMARY_015: After losing their devil powers, Jin and Kazuya are left to finish their epic family conflict as mere humans. Jin, shouldering the hopes of his allies, utilizes every technique he had acquired until now. Kazuya too fights Jin with the entirety of his being, refusing to have his way of existence dictated by anyone. The two exchange fists of iron that weigh heavy with their resolve, until the last blow is dealt. And the last man left standing was...
TEXT_008_UI_STORY_DIFFICULTY_TEXT_001:

*The difficulty setting is the same for Story, Episodes, and Character Episodes.
